title: Haitian man living in Boston dies in ICE custody, heightening concerns about detention centers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Emmanuel Damas, a 56-year-old Haitian asylum seeker in Boston, died in ICE custody after inadequate treatment for a tooth infection.
Lawmakers criticized ICE's medical care, citing Damas's death as part of broader patterns of neglect in detention centers.
Damas's death raises questions about ICE detention conditions; his family claims his toothache led to fatal sepsis.

title: Celtics officially list Jayson Tatum as ‘Available’ for game vs. Mavericks
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum is listed as 'available' for the Celtics' game against the Mavericks after being out since 2025.
Tatum, who ruptured his Achilles in the 2025 playoffs, was initially listed as 'questionable' before being upgraded.
The Celtics, currently second in the Eastern Conference, face roster adjustments with Tatum's return against the Mavericks.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to two-year deal with backup QB Tommy DeVito
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots sign QB Tommy DeVito to a two-year, $7.4 million deal with $2 million guaranteed.
DeVito, 27, became known as 'Tommy Cutlets' during his time with the Giants in 2023.
Patriots roster DeVito as a backup to starter Drake Maye, alongside veteran Josh Dobbs.

title: South Shore mother and son stuck in Qatar amid war in Iran
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Stacey Schuhwerk and her son, from Hingham, are stranded in Qatar due to halted Middle East flights.
U.S. Rep. Stephen Lynch criticized the Trump administration's lack of evacuation efforts before strikes on Iran.
Lynch states U.S. officials promised more flights to bring Americans home amid continued military actions.

title: Tufts announces new proposed aquatics center for swimming and diving teams
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tufts University plans a new aquatics center to replace the 80-year-old Hamilton Pool, pending City of Medford approval.
The center will feature a 50-by-25-meter pool, diving boards, and facilities for hosting conference and regional competitions.
Tufts has raised $21.7 million for the project, with construction aimed for completion by fall 2028 semester.

title: Circle Furniture to auction off remaining inventory after bankruptcy
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Circle Furniture is auctioning its remaining inventory online following bankruptcy, with auctions running from March 19 to April 29.
The company, founded in 1952, closed in December, affecting 65 employees, and has liabilities of nearly $14 million.
Store locations in Massachusetts and New Hampshire will hold separate auctions for items like furniture and store decor.

title: Watch the N.H. Liquor & Wine Outlet video that’s lowkey going viral
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A video from New Hampshire Liquor & Wine Outlet went viral, receiving over a million views on Facebook and Instagram.
The video features marketing director Mark Roy using Gen Z and TikTok phrases to attract younger consumers to wine and spirits.
The outlet also offers in-person events like 'Sommelier Select' to engage younger audiences, with these events quickly selling out.

title: Bruins trade deadline live updates: Will Don Sweeney add or subtract from Boston’s roster?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Don Sweeney and the Boston Bruins face a decision on buying or selling ahead of the NHL trade deadline.
The Bruins traded Brett Harrison and Jackson Edward for Alexis Gendron and Massimo Rizzo from the Flyers.
Boston sits one point ahead of the Columbus Blue Jackets for a playoff spot as the deadline approaches.

title: Job interviews now include AI and personality tests. Here’s what candidates should know.
author: Elaine Varelas
bullets: Modern job interviews increasingly include AI assessments and personality tests to predict candidates' success and fit.
AI-driven interview tools analyzing traits like integrity have faced legal issues under Massachusetts law.
82% of companies use assessment tools, raising concerns about bias and the importance of transparent hiring processes.

title: She’s running her 11th Boston Marathon for her son and others affected by addiction
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jill Borthwick, 72, from Freemont, NH, will run her 11th Boston Marathon in 2026.
Borthwick is running to honor her son, John O'Brien, and others affected by addiction.
She supports the Herren Project, inspired by the film 'Unguarded,' as a tribute to her late son.
